label: 242_white population_white_population_2020 census
description: The 2020 census data showed slower growth for the white population and an increase in diversity across states. The youth population declined, while people of color became more highly represented.

earliest_articles.title: Five Myths About the 2010 Census and the U.S. Population
earliest_articles.excerpt: Every 10 years, we have to count people. At least that’s what Article 1, Section 2 of the Constitution says. It doesn’t sound too complicated. But it is. Who gets counted, and how, determines not only congressional representation but how funding is distributed for a slew of federal programs that affect all of us. As…

earliest_articles.title: A Demographic Tipping Point Among America’s Three-Year-Olds
earliest_articles.excerpt: This past Monday the Census Bureau released its new statistics on the nation’s children and school enrollment, and it showed something momentous. For the first time since this annual data series has been released, fewer than half of all the children (49.9 percent) in the youngest age group shown, three-year-olds, were…

earliest_articles.title: @ Brookings Podcast: The Census and Changing Demographics in U.S. Schools
earliest_articles.excerpt: The 2010 Census Bureau confirms what demographers have long predicted: a dramatic shift in the racial makeup of American population. Fewer than half of the nation’s 3-year olds are Caucasian, supporting the premise that the America of tomorrow will be comprised of a minority majority. Preparation for the challenges…

newest_articles.title: New 2020 census data shows an aging America and wide racial gaps between generations
newest_articles.excerpt: This report is part of a series by the author on the results of the 2020 census. In April 2021, the Census Bureau released the first set of results from the 2020 decennial census, providing a snapshot of the U.S. population for use in congressional reapportionment and redistricting. But recently, the agency released…

newest_articles.title: White and youth population losses contributed most to the nation’s growth slowdown, new census data reveals
newest_articles.excerpt: Previous analyses of Census Bureau estimates make plain that the nation’s population growth has ground down to a historic low: only 0.1% growth between July 2020 and July 2021. During this prime year of the COVID-19 pandemic, the number of deaths rose sharply, births declined, and immigration reached its lowest levels…

central_articles.title: Census shows America’s post-2020 population is driven by diversity especially among the young
central_articles.excerpt: Last month, the Census Bureau released new data for geographic areas which provide a glimpse of how the nation’s population has been changing over the first third of the 2020s decade. It emphasizes that people of color are the main drivers of population growth for the nation as well as shifts across states,…

central_articles.title: New 2020 census results show increased diversity countering decade-long declines in America’s white and youth populations
central_articles.excerpt: This report is part of a series by the author on the results of the 2020 census.
